metadata.description: Master plant database for 3-year food forest succession — canopy first
metadata.successionPrinciple: Canopy → Sub-canopy → Shrubs → Herbaceous → Ground cover

year0.name: Canopy & Infrastructure
year0.description: Plant overstory trees immediately. They need 5–15 years to mature. Everything else is designed around them.
year0.timingRule: Bare root planting in late winter/early spring dormancy. Waning Moon for root focus.

plants.name: Black Walnut (Juglans nigra)
plants.notes: Juglone-tolerant guild required: elderberry, pawpaw, currants, hazelnuts, raspberries

plants.name: American Chestnut (Castanea dentata hybrid)
plants.notes: Use blight-resistant hybrids (Dunstan, Colossal). Starchy staple crop.

plants.name: Pecan (Carya illinoinensis)
plants.notes: Deep taproot brings up subsoil minerals. Requires pollinator variety.

plants.name: American Persimmon (Diospyros virginiana)
plants.notes: Taprooted, drought-tolerant once established. Male + female required.

year1.name: Sub-canopy & Shrubs
year1.description: Fruit trees, berry bushes, and woody nitrogen fixers planted into the developing canopy microclimate.
year1.timingRule: Dormant season planting. Waxing Moon for establishment vigor.

plants.name: Apple (Malus pumila)
plants.notes: Choose disease-resistant varieties. Guild with comfrey, chives, daffodils, clover.

plants.name: Pear (Pyrus communis)
plants.notes: Fire blight resistant varieties preferred. Partially self-fertile.

plants.name: Peach (Prunus persica)
plants.notes: Short-lived (15-20 yrs). Replace as canopy matures.

plants.name: Autumn Olive (Elaeagnus umbellata)
plants.notes: Chop-and-drop biomass producer. Invasive in some regions — check local regs.

plants.name: Black Currant (Ribes nigrum)
plants.notes: WPBR resistant varieties. Shade tolerant — perfect understory.

plants.name: Red Raspberry (Rubus idaeus)
plants.notes: Summer or everbearing. Forms dense thickets.

plants.name: Fox Grape (Vitis labrusca)
plants.notes: Native alternative to European wine grapes. Muscadine in South.

year2.name: Herbaceous, Ground Cover & Guild Completion
year2.description: Fill in between rows and under trees with dynamic accumulators, insectaries, and perennial vegetables.
year2.timingRule: Spring after last frost. Waxing Moon for leafy establishment.

plants.name: Russian Comfrey (Symphytum x uplandicum 'Bocking 14')
plants.notes: Plant at tree drip lines. Chop 4-5x/season for mulch.

plants.name: Yarrow (Achillea millefolium)
plants.notes: BD 502-507 compost preparation plant. Drought-tolerant.

plants.name: Asparagus (Asparagus officinalis)
plants.notes: Plant on berms for drainage. 20+ year lifespan.

plants.name: Jerusalem Artichoke (Helianthus tuberosus)
plants.notes: Invasive spread — plant in contained beds or mow perimeter.

plants.name: American Hazelnut (Corylus americana)
plants.notes: Juglone tolerant — good black walnut companion. 3-5 yrs to production.

plants.name: French Sorrel (Rumex acetosa)
plants.notes: Lemony perennial spinach. Cut-and-come-again harvest.

plants.name: Chives (Allium schoenoprasum)
plants.notes: Apple guild standard. Repels apple maggot and scab.

plants.name: White Clover (Trifolium repens)
plants.notes: Walk-on ground cover between rows. Mow or let flower.

supportSpecies.name: Nitrogen Fixers & Dynamic Accumulators (Chop-and-Drop)
supportSpecies.description: These species are planted throughout all years and maintained as biomass producers, not primary harvests.

plants.name: Sea Buckthorn (Hippophae rhamnoides)
plants.notes: Male + female required for berries. Extremely hardy.

plants.name: Goumi (Elaeagnus multiflora)
plants.notes: Self-fertile. Red tart berries. Drought-tolerant once established.

plants.name: Stinging Nettle (Urtica dioica)
plants.notes: High protein biomass. Compost activator. Medicinal tea.

preparations.BD500: Horn manure - root stimulation, Waning Moon
preparations.BD501: Horn silica - foliar spray, Waxing Moon
preparations.BD502-507: Compost preparations - Yarrow, Chamomile, Nettle, Oak Bark, Dandelion, Valerian
